text.title: Agentic Commerce Implementation
text.summary: Four implementation areas for agentic commerce readiness: earned media strategy, task-completion content design, cross-platform entity consistency, and multi-format content presence.

rule.statement: Build earned media through industry publication features, original research, expert commentary, and journalist relationships. This is the highest-weight factor in AI agent provider selection.
rule.rationale: AI agents cross-reference multiple sources when evaluating providers. A business mentioned positively in authoritative publications across multiple platforms scores higher than one with excellent SEO but no earned coverage.

rule.statement: Collect authentic reviews across all relevant platforms (G2, Capterra, Google, Yelp, TrustRadius as appropriate) and maintain above 4.5/5 average. Respond professionally to all feedback.
rule.rationale: AI systems cross-check review authenticity by comparing patterns across platforms. Fake reviews are detectable; authentic reviews from real customers at scale are not. Review score and volume are directly weighted in AI agent provider selection.

rule.statement: Create content organized around user tasks, not keywords. Identify the specific tasks your customers complete (e.g., "book emergency plumber," "find CRM for 50-person sales team") and build dedicated task-oriented pages.
rule.rationale: AI agents match user intent to provider based on task-completion language. Keyword-optimized content ("best plumber in city") does not map to conversational AI queries ("fix my leak today"). Task-oriented content maps directly.

rule.statement: Ensure identical NAP (Name, Address, Phone) and company description across Google Business Profile, all social media profiles, directory listings, and knowledge panels.
rule.rationale: AI entity recognition depends on consistent information across sources. Inconsistent descriptions confuse entity resolution; an AI agent that cannot reliably identify your entity will de-weight you relative to competitors with consistent signals.

antipattern.description: Different company descriptions on each platform — slightly different service list, different founding year, different tagline.
antipattern.reason: Inconsistency signals unreliability to AI entity recognition systems. Even minor inconsistencies (abbreviated vs. full company name) reduce confidence in entity identification.

rule.statement: Maintain content presence across text (website), video (YouTube), audio (podcast), and structured data (schema, APIs). AI systems pull from diverse source types.
rule.rationale: AI agents aggregate information from multiple formats and sources. Text-only presence limits the number of signals an AI can use to evaluate your business. Video and audio provide additional authority signals for AI systems that index multimedia.

text.reference: Phased checklist for agentic commerce preparation
